meta.hypothesis: Spawning the vite dev server and driving the webgpu-dna harness in headless Chromium produces the first energy result in #tb within ~90 seconds at N=1024 primaries / E=100 eV (lowest ESTAR energy). The CSDA value in that row is plausible (> 1 nm, < 50000 nm).
meta.passBar: first row appears within 90s AND CSDA cell parses to a number in [1, 50000] nm AND no page-level errors during the run.
meta.seed: n/a (harness uses its own RNG; this experiment only checks liveness)

shaderHashesRetrofit.note: Retrofit by tools/retrofit-shader-hashes.mjs — original artifact predated the shaderHashes audit fix (2026-05-12).

summary.headline: first row (E=100 eV) in 2.9s; CSDA=15.7 nm (1 rows captured)

rows.logTail: [13:58:01] GPU initialized: [13:58:01] Shaders compiled, buffers allocated (np=1024)[13:58:01] Running 1024 primaries at 8 energies, box=15000nm, cutoff=7.4eV[13:58:01] DNA target: 441 fibers × 8823 bp = 3.89 Mbp (3000 nm, 21² grid, 150 nm spacing)[13:58:02]   E=100 eV: CSDA=15.7nm (6.54×) ions/pri=3.8 sec/pri=1.9 G_init(OH/eaq/H)=5.79/4.57/1.33 rad_n=16749 E_cons=99.9%
